pure_fr.evidence: Output A refines the FR into multiple sub-FRs, improving atomicity and testability, but the top-level FR is not fully atomic and still somewhat broad. Output B presents a fully atomic, solution-neutral FR with clear single obligation and measurable acceptance criteria, explicitly stopping decomposition before prescribing solution details, thus better satisfying pure FR criteria.

fr_dp_bridge.evidence: Output A demonstrates strong adherence to the Independence Axiom by explicitly decomposing DP5 into three cohesive sub-modules with clear interfaces, minimizing coupling and complexity. It also addresses the what-vs-how boundary well by keeping the DP logical and solution-neutral while recommending architectural refinements. Output B shows a sound FR-DP mapping with high containment and clear interfaces but has slightly less explicit internal decomposition and some residual coupling risks (e.g., SafetyAlert interface evolution, real-time scheduling) that are acknowledged but less mitigated.

dp_im_bridge.evidence: Output A demonstrates excellent containment of DP within a single Teleoperation IM boundary with a high containment score (0.92) and minimal diffusion (0.08), supported by explicit identification of internal sub-modules to manage complexity without scattering DP logic. Output B also shows high containment (0.95) with minimal diffusion (0.05) but lacks the same level of detailed internal decomposition and explicit mitigation of diffusion risks, resulting in slightly less architectural clarity on managing complexity within the IM.

pure_im.evidence: Output A provides detailed module containment scores with a high Q-score (0.92) and explicit internal sub-module decomposition recommendations to maintain cohesion and manage complexity within a single IM boundary. It includes DSM-like containment/diffusion metrics and clear architectural rationale. Output B also shows high containment (0.95) and recommends sub-module decomposition but lacks detailed structural metrics or explicit modularity optimization evidence, resulting in slightly lower confidence in structural modularity.

fr_im_bridge.evidence: Both outputs clearly allocate the FR to the Teleoperation IM with high containment scores (0.95 and 0.92) and well-defined external interfaces. They provide strong verifiability by defining measurable acceptance criteria for link availability and testability via continuous monitoring. Output B further details internal sub-module decomposition and interface formalization, enhancing architectural clarity and verification feasibility, but both meet the FR-IM bridge criteria effectively.
